texts.full_text: live kidney donation. Thus, many centers are interested in adopting this procedure. However, the high stakes involved for both the donor and the recipient, and the technical difficulties of the operation, have tempered the enthusiasm of some surgeons. Ideally, if early in their series, surgeons could select patients that would be the least challenging technically, it would facilitate the dissemination of this operation. The purpose of this study is to determine if anatomic or radiologic parameters can accurately assess the technical difficulty of laparoscopic live donor nephrectomy (LLDN) pre-operatively. This would allow surgeons to start their series with lower-risk cases, thereby increasing the likelihood of success and reducing potential complications. To achieve this, we propose a retrospective review of patients who underwent LLDN at your institution. We will analyze various anatomic and radiologic parameters, such as: 1. Kidney size: Larger kidneys may be more challenging to mobilize and extract through a small incision. 2. Kidney position: Lower pole kidneys may be more difficult to mobilize due to their proximity to the iliac vessels. 3. texts.full_text: on, withdrawal, and difficulty forming secure relationships. These findings align with the hYpothesis that children with RAD exhibit disrupted internal working models (IWMs), which manifest as negative self-perceptions and distrust of others. The study also revealed that children in the RAD risk group demonstrated lower self-concept scores, particularly in domains as social competence, academic self-efficacy, and emotional stability. Observational data indicated that these children displayed more frequent signs of negative IWMs, including behaviors, reduced eye contact during social interactions, and limited responsiveness to caregiver emotional cues. Notably, the association between reported RAD symptoms and observed behavioral patterns was strongest in children with confirmed disruptions in early histories (e.g., history of instItutional care or multiple caregiver transitions). The study also highlighted that mental health outcomes, such as elevated anxiety and depressive symptoms, were more prevalent in the RAD risk group, underscoring the interplay between attachment disruptions and psychosocial functioning. These findings emphasize the importance of integrating both caregiver reports and observational methods to comprehensively assess attachment-related challenges in middle childhood. The results suggest that interventions targeting the reinforcement of positive IWMs—through consistent caregiving and structured emotional regulation support—could mitigate long-term risks for maladaptive self-concept and relational difficulties. However, the authors caution that further research is needed to clarify causal pathways and to develop culturally sensitive tools for early identification of RAD risk factors. --- This completion synthesizes the study's methodology, results, and implications while maintaining academic rigor and coherence with the original text. texts.full_text: preservation, with radial forearm free flap (RFFF) reconstruction. Retrospective review of our experience using RFFF reconstruction in 9 patients. All 9 patients had a posterior pharyngectomy with larynx preservation, neck dissection (3 bilateral and 6 bilateral), and reconstructive surgery utilizing the radial forearm free flap for soft tissue coverage. Patient demographics included ages ranging from 45 to 72 years old. The primary diagnoses were squamous cell carcinoma in all cases, with varying stages of disease as follows: stage T1N0M0 (n=3), T2aN0-1M0 (n=2), and locally advanced unresectable or recurrent lesions requiring salvage surgery (T4a/b) for the remaining 4 patients. The surgical technique involved a posterior pharyngectomy with preservation of laryngeal function, followed by thorough neck dissection to address lymphadenopathy. The RFFF was harvested using standard microvascular techniques and then meticulously fashioned into an appropriate shape and size before being inset at the resected site in the oropharynx. Postoperative complications were noted in 4 patients (44%), including one case of flap necrosis that required debridement, two instances of partial thrombosis managed conservatively with anticoagulation therapy, and a single occurrence of tracheostomy decannulation failure necessitating prolonged ventilatory support. Despite these challenges, all nine patients achieved successful larynx preservation. At the time of last follow-up (ranging from 12 to 36 months), five patients remained disease-free without evidence of recurrence on imaging studies and clinical examination; two experienced local recurrences that were managed with salvage radiation therapy alone or in combination with brachytherapy, while one patient developed distant metastasis leading to systemic treatment. The overall survival rate was 89%, highlighting the potential for favorable outcomes when employing this surgical approach. In conclusion, our preliminary report suggests that posterior pharyngectomy combined with RFFF reconstruction is a viable option for selected patients requiring larynx preservation surgery. Further

texts.full_text: nshei Knesset Hagedola returning the description of “Awe” as an appropriate way to describe and praise G-d. Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi explains on our daf in detail the process and reason for restoring this description of G-d. MosRabbi Yehoshua ben Levi elaborates that during the period of the Babylonian exile, when the Jewish people were under great duress and faced significant challenges to their survival as a distinct nation, they questioned how it was possible for them to endure among so many other nations without being assimilated or destroyed. The answer he provides is rooted in the concept of "awesomeness" (גאווה) of G-d. The Anshei Knesset Hagedola, the Men of the Great Assembly, who were a group of sages and prophets that led the Jewish people during the early Second Temple period, recognized this attribute as essential to understanding their survival. They reinstated the use of "Awe" (יִרְאָה) in describing G-d because it encapsulates both fear and respect for His power and justice. This restoration was not just a theological adjustment but also a practical affirmation that despite the hardships, the Jewish people’s continued existence is a testament to G-d's protective presence. The awe of G-d serves as a reminder of the divine providence that has sustained them through generations of adversity. A clinical case of a 54-year-old patient is presented. After injection of hyaluronic acid in the treatment of nasolabial folds elsewhere, she developed palpable painful erythematous nodules evolving into abscesses several month after injection. Surgical treatment and correction of these lesions after hyaluronic acid injection of the nasolabial folds and the histological finding...of granulomatous inflammation with foreign body reaction were observed, confirming the diagnosis of severe granulomatous allergic tissue reaction to hyaluronic acid. texts.full_text: had a fascinating discussion with Republican Congressman Steve King that clearly revealed the rift in the Republican Party over immigration and outreach to minorities. But one thing they were in synch about was their disdain for Hispanics which was made crystal clear, whether they realized it or not. The discussion was recorded yesterday, so O’Reilly did not have the chance to address how King has since used the Boston bombings to justify opposing immigration reform. BuT King was already against it when he visited The O’Reilly Factor and O’Reilly tried to persuade him that it’s time for the GOP to “bend.” He added, “Every single poll shows 75% of the Hispanic voters are leaning Democrats because of this issue. Every poll says that!” King arKing argued that the GOP should not change its stance to cater to Hispanic voters, maintaining that the party should stick to its principles. He contended that the party should not be swayed by poll numbers, suggesting that principles are more important than electoral strategy. King’s stance reflects a hardline position that is at odds with the more pragmatic approach advocated by O’Reilly, who sees the demographic shift and the importance of appealing to a broader electorate, including Hispanic voters, as a critical path forward for the GOP. O’Reilly’s frustration with King’s intransigence is palpable, as he sees the Republican Party’s future success as being closely tied to its ability to diversify its base

texts.full_text: diameter of 6.5 cm. The aneurysm was located in the infrarenal aorta and showed signs of active inflammation and surrounding soft tissue edema. The patient's clinical condition deteriorated rapidly, and she was transferred to a tertiary care center for further management. Upon arrival, the patient was hemodynamically unstable, with a blood pressure of 85/55 mmHg and a heart rate of 120 beats per minute. She was immediately started on broad-spectrum antibiotics, including piperacillin-tazobactam and vancomycin, and was taken to the operating room for emergency repair of the mycotic aneurysm. The surgical team opted for an open repair, given the patient's critical condition and the need for debridement of the infected tissue. The aneurysm was excised, and a graft was placed to restore aortic continuity. The surgical site was meticulously debrided, and a thorough washout was performed to reduce the bacterial load. Postoperatively, the patient was admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU) and continued on intravenous antibiotics. Her recovery was complicated by acute kidney injury, likely secondary to the perioperative hypotension, and she required temporary hemodialysis. Despite the complications, she gradually improved and was eventually weaned off hemodialysis. Her antibiotic course was continued for six weeks, and she was closely monitored for signs of infection recurrence. 3. texts.full_text: dele Lewing Adele Lewing (6 August 1866 – 16 February 1943) was a German pianist and composer. Early years and education Lewing was born in Hanover in 1868. She was educated in classical music by her grandfather, A. C. Prell, the principal cellist in the , a pupil of Bernhard Romberg; and in piano by Johannes Moeller, a pupil of Ignaz Moscheles. At the age of 14, she made her first public appearance. Later, she became the student of Carl Reinecke and Salomon Jadassohn at the Leipzig conservatory, studying also harmony with the latter. Reinecke selected Lewing to play Mendelssohn's first Cello Sonata in the Mendelssohn celebration, and she was also chosen to play the F minor suite by Handel in a concert in honor of the King of Saxony. On 30 April 1884 Lewing played Beethoven's concerto in G major, on her first appearance in the public examination in the Leipzig Gewandhaus. Reinecke selected Lewing to play his quintet, Op. 82, in another concert on 10 May 1884. However, by the time of the fourth war, the British Royal Navy had The British Royal Navy had significantly expanded and modernized, thanks to the efforts of naval reformers like Admiral Robert Blake and the investments of the British government. The power of the British navy was further solidified by the leadership of Admiral Sir George Rodney during the Fourth Anglo-Dutch War (1780-1784), which was part of the larger Seven Years' War. During this conflict, the British navy demonstrated its superiority with decisive victories, most notably at the Battle of the Saintes in 1782. This battle saw the British fleet under Admiral Rodney engage and defeat a French and Spanish fleet, which was a significant setback for the Dutch-allied forces. The British victory at the Battle of the Saintes is often cited as a turning point in the Fourth Anglo-Dutch War and the wider Seven Years' War, as it effectively sealed the fate of the Dutch Republic in the conflict. The outcome of the Fourth Anglo-Dutch War further solidified British naval dominance and contributed to the decline of Dutch maritime power. The Treaty of Paris, which ended the war in 1784, confirmed British control over several strategic naval bases and trade routes, further enhancing their global maritime supremacy. texts.full_text: for the intermittent duty of a consumer camera and are included with professional video cameras primarily to reduce the overall manufactured cost. The batteries included with known handheld video cameras often define a footprint (length and width) for their mounting means that is itself small in area, and therefore only allows for extending the battery outwardly from the camera body, resulting in an awkward and mostly unusable dimension. Also, the footprint does not allow for the increase in the size of the cells used in the battery. Therefore, improving the performance or quality of the battery is virtually impossible. The reduction in size and weight of known handheld video cameras also creates a problem in the physics behind the ability of a pan and tilt head to properly control the movement of the camera. As the camera mass is lessened, the ability of the head to dampen unwanted movements made by the operator is also lessened. Moreover, the light weight of smaller video cameras actually requires at least as heavy a tripod base in order to provide a stable shooting platform. Thus, the reduction in size and weight of handheld cameras has caused a problem for both the power source and the stabilizing devices typically used, especially by professionals, to operate the camera to generate quality video productions. Ironically, both designers of stabilizing devices and designers of power sources have been forced to add at least as much weight to the operating system—camera, battery, head and tripod—as the camera manufacturer eliminated in reducing the size and overall weight of video camera devices in the first place. texts.full_text: and how many times you have gotten a number from the generator so that you get exactly the same results out. Since we are talking about a single core you don't have to worry too much about order of evaluation which is good, but make sure it is well defined. Second it will be easiest if you use a MVC(model view controller) setup then all you need is a playback controller and a live user controller that records timestamped input if necessary. Note: depending on how you do it it may be easier to use a frame count rather than an actual time. When the player hits record, gather the entire state of the game, and start capturing player input. To export video all you have to do is load the starting state playback a frame, capture the frame then iterate over the entire playback record. If you have things in your game that are time based you will have to fiddle with the clock so that time spent doing the video capture is removed. Alternatively you could record time based events with a frame count so that it happens at the right frame, but then you have to make sure the different processing order doesn't change the game. A second option is to go event based, just record every event that happens in the game Then during playback just trigger those events during the frame they originally happened in. This requires capturing more data since you aren't using the game to simulate everything(you still need to do animations and such), but might improve render times as you aren't running the whole game.

Housed in a historic house (built c. 1866) on the western edge of campus, the department is home to roughly one hundred majors and one hundred minors. You'll discover a wide range of course offerings spanning ethics, Chinese philosophy, logic, philosophy of religion, philosophy of language, philosophy of mind, aetaphysics, continental philosophy, social and political philosophy, philosophy of law, and ancient and modern philosophy. Philosophy: Fundamental to Fulfilling Careers Eric Lipton '87, longtime reporter for the New York Times and a philosophy major at UVM, received a Pulitzer Prize in 2015 for investigative reporting. "UVM offered a place in the philosophy department where I was forced to think. The honors thesis I did at the time was one of the hardest things I did in my life. texts.full_text: n Archive Dir agents: linux, aix, solaris, hpux catalog: app/libelle license: GPL distribution: check_mk description: Checks the total / used capacity in the Archive Dir of Libelle Business Shadow. Returns {WARN} or {CRITif conditions are met based on thresholds set for warning and critical levels. The plugin checks disk usage statistics specifically within a designated archive directory where data from business shadowing is stored, ensuring that storage space does not become an issue affecting system performance due to lack of available capacity. ### Implementation Details - Data Collection: Utilizes standard Linux commands like `du` or filesystem utilities specific for AIX (`df`, etc.), Solaris (e.g., using `/usr/sbin/zfs list -H --long <zpool_name>`), and HP/UX systems, to gather data on directory sizes. - Thresholds Configuration: Users can define thresholds at which the system will trigger a warning or critical alert. These are typically set as percentages of total capacity used (e.g., WARN if >70% is utilized; CRIT when exceeding <10%). ### Usage To integrate this check into your monitoring setup, you should: 1. texts.full_text: 61.9 45,077 **India** **80.9** **48.5** **160,550** Source: IIPS, 2010. The knowledge and awareness on reproductive health issues among unmarried women were also collected in the DLHS-3. On an average, the women who received FLE had much better awareness on various reproductive health issues like RTI/STI, possibility of finding out the sex of a baby before birth, and knowledge about reducing chances of infecting HIV as compared with women who did not receive any FLE ([**Table 4**](#pone-0071584-t004){ref-type="table"}). In general, women who received the FLE were relatively more aware about methods of contraception as compared to their counterparts. For instance, among women who received FLE, nearly 98 and 27 percent of women were aware about any modern and traditional methods of contraception respectively.
